text:
Market today: Mixed signals in chip-land. Micron's blowout earnings lit up the memory/AI chip trade, but Apple dropping 5% on a memory crunch-driven price hike is a real overhang - those two stories together tell you the AI chip demand is intense but costs are biting downstream. Your tech-heavy, margin book is sitting right in the crossfire.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest alarm - down 17.86% and on a SELL signal; on margin that hole deepens fast. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.32% with a TRIM flag - a falling anchor drags everything. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; tiny move to your $150 stop and you're out - margin amplifies that instantly.

Watch: 1) Does AVGO stabilize or follow Apple's memory-cost pain lower - it's your biggest dollar risk. 2) How far Micron's earnings glow spreads to SMH, your one green chip name.

Bottom line: Trim or cut your two biggest losers (PLTR, AVGO) before margin math makes the decision for you.
